verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (UniProt P54107) is a classical secreted glycoprotein of the CAP/CRISP family. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no known covalent or wash-resistant link to any TM partner. It is expressed in the epididymis, secreted into the epididymal lumen, and reversibly adsorbs onto the postacrosomal region of the sperm head surface — an interaction that is lost during capacitation. This non-covalent, reversible binding to the sperm surface fails the wash test and does not constitute surface anchoring via the protein's own mechanism. No therapeutic surface-targeting programs (ADC, CAR-T, bispecific) targeting CRISP1 on the cell surface are documented. Verdict: no / secreted_only.
